content.problemStatement:
Post-order traversal involves visiting the left subtree first, then the right subtree, and finally the root node. This method is useful for deleting the tree and freeing up the space, as it ensures that child nodes are processed before their respective parent nodes.

**Algorithm**:


Recursively traverse the left subtree.
Recursively traverse the right subtree.
Visit the root node and print it.
Video Explanation
For example, check the postorder traversal of the following binary tree.



TaskGiven a binary tree, print its nodes in postorder traversal.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of nodes in the binary tree.
Next N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 lines contain three space separated characters pi,ci,Rip_i, c_i, R_ipi‚Äã,ci‚Äã,Ri‚Äã, describing the tree with edge informations - pip_ipi‚Äã is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, cic_ici‚Äã is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi‚Äã denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if cic_ici‚Äã is left child of pip_ipi‚Äã, else `R`.


Output Format
Output on the single line, NNN space separated integers - its nodes in the postorder traversal of the given tree.
constraints: All pip_ipi‚Äã's and cic_ici‚Äã's are distinct.
